operation.label: Trigger activation
operation.description: Activate a trigger and patch all affected downstream dependencies.

affected_closure.label: A bounded diligence authorization is supportable only if the move remains inquiry-only, reversible, and visibly separate from operational launch.
Operational launch remains blocked until release criteria, reviewer authority, and approval evidence are all present.
A named release authority is required before launch can move from blocked to approved.
approve bounded diligence as an inquiry move only; keep launch blocked until evidence and named authority close the release gate
What specific evidence checklist, threshold, and owner would convert the diligence cycle into a launch/no-launch decision?
Gate escalation: diligence creates irreversible launch exposure, public commitment, or material spend before approval.
Adapter conflict or stakeholder objection shows the proposed reviewer lacks legitimate authority over launch release.

Proceed with bounded diligence under written guardrails. Do not launch, externally commit, or spend irreversibly until the evidence package is complete and G1 is approved by the named release authority.
one bounded diligence cycle before launch
The repeat-3 map still lacks a concrete diligence evidence package, launch threshold, and pass/fail criteria.

changed_decision_meaning: Trigger activation changes T1:trigger; 29 downstream object(s) are in closure, so the current decision rendering must be regenerated.

semantic_patch_operations.effect: Activate a trigger and patch all affected downstream dependencies.
Recompute support, contradiction load, confidence, and failure modes.
Render a new decision view after validation passes.

patch_report.summary: trigger_activation on T1 affects 29 downstream object(s); transition=patch; rendering=affected
next_steps: rerun validation after applying any object edits
reassess affected claims: C1, C2, C4
record trigger state change in patch history or review log
